scenarios.description: Minimum-cost prompt: no tool use, one-word reply
scenarios.query: Say the word 'ok' and nothing else.
Read the top 40 lines of /opt/hermes/README.md and summarize the quick-install flow in one sentence.
Run the shell command `python3 --version` and report the output verbatim.

scenarios.description: search_files + 3× read_file(full) + synthesis — drives realistic tool-result growth
scenarios.query: Search /opt/hermes for files containing the string 'openai-compat-v2'. Then read the FULL contents of the 3 most relevant matches. Then write a 3-sentence summary of how openai-compat-v2 is referenced across the codebase.

scenarios.description: git log + 2× git show + synthesis — exercises terminal with large outputs
scenarios.query: Run `git -C /opt/hermes log -10 --stat --no-color` to see recent commits. Pick the 2 most interesting commits by stat size and run `git -C /opt/hermes show <hash> --stat --no-color` on each. Then write a 2-paragraph summary of what those commits changed.
Read /opt/hermes/README.md, /opt/hermes/CONTRIBUTING.md, and /opt/hermes/SECURITY.md in full. Then write a comparison: what audience does each target, and how do they overlap?

scenarios.description: search + iterative read + structured summary — the longest expected scenario
scenarios.query: Audit the directory /opt/hermes/agent for Python files that import 'openai' directly. Use search_files to list candidates, then read each match's top 30 lines, then write a 1-paragraph summary of the import patterns. Limit to 5 files to keep the task bounded.
